meta.notes: Psychology (PSBB) items scraped verbatim from OpenStax Psychology 2e Ch. 2 review-questions page; correct answers taken from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), which publishes answers to odd-numbered review questions only (so only odd-numbered items are included). The live OpenStax page shows a CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 license plus a no-LLM-training notice; per the SpeedyCAT PRD this content is used human-facing and non-AI, mirroring psbb-001 in questions.json. Q7 omitted: the Solutions Guide left its answer blank.

questions.explanation: A scientific hypothesis must be both testable and falsifiable, meaning it can be supported or refuted by empirical evidence. 'Provable' overstates the case, because hypotheses are supported rather than definitively proven.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 2 Q1 = D.

questions.explanation: Scientific knowledge is empirical: it is grounded in objective, systematic observation and measurement rather than in intuition or subjective opinion.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 2 Q3 = B.

questions.stem: Sigmund Freud developed his theory of human personality by conducting in-depth interviews over an extended period of time with a few clients. This type of research approach is known as a(n): ________.
questions.explanation: Studying a small number of individuals in great depth over an extended period is a case study. Freud built his personality theory from such intensive study of a few clients.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 2 Q5 = B.

questions.stem: Using existing records to try to answer a research question is known as ________.
questions.explanation: Archival research uses pre-existing records or data sets (such as historical documents or databases) to answer a research question.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 2 Q9 = D.

questions.stem: A(n) ________ is a list of questions developed by a researcher that can be administered in paper form.
questions.explanation: A survey is a list of questions, administered on paper or electronically, used to gather self-report data from many respondents.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax (option B casing 'case Study' as printed). correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 2 Q11 = D.

questions.stem: Height and weight are positively correlated. This means that:

choices.label: A
choices.text: There is no relationship between height and weight.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Usually, the taller someone is, the thinner they are.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Usually, the shorter someone is, the heavier they are.

choices.label: D
choices.text: As height increases, typically weight increases.

questions.explanation: A positive correlation means two variables move in the same direction: as one increases, the other typically increases too. So taller people tend to weigh more.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 2 Q13 = D.

questions.stem: Which statement best illustrates a negative correlation between the number of hours spent watching TV the week before an exam and the grade on that exam?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Watching too much television leads to poor exam performance.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Viewing television interferes with a student's ability to prepare for the upcoming exam.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Students who watch more television perform more poorly on their exams.

questions.explanation: A correlation describes a relationship without claiming causation. Only choice D simply states the inverse relationship; the other options imply that TV viewing causes poor performance, which a correlation cannot establish.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 2 Q15 = D.

questions.stem: ________ means that everyone in the population has the same likelihood of being asked to participate in the study.
questions.explanation: Random sampling means every member of the population has an equal chance of being selected to participate, which supports generalizability. Random assignment, by contrast, sorts already-selected participants into groups.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 2 Q17 = D.

questions.stem: Researchers must ________ important concepts in their studies so others would have a clear understanding of exactly how those concepts were defined.
questions.explanation: To operationalize a concept is to define it in terms of specific, measurable operations, so others know exactly how each variable was measured.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 2 Q19 = C.

questions.stem: ________ is to animal research as ________ is to human research.
questions.explanation: An IACUC (Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee) reviews animal research, while an IRB (Institutional Review Board) reviews research involving human participants.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 2 Q21 = B.

questions.stem: A person's participation in a research project must be ________.
questions.explanation: Ethical research requires that participation be voluntary: people must freely choose to take part (and may withdraw at any time) without coercion.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
